channel.title: Migration on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Migration on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Expanding Azure Arc SQL Migration with SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure Arc SQL migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a target. Arc-enabled SQL Server instances can be migrated to either Azure SQL Managed Instance or SQL Server on Azure infrastructure using the same migration solution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes Dashboard to Headlamp: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description:
Kubernetes Dashboard users can migrate to Headlamp by choosing either a desktop or in-cluster deployment. The guide covers kubeconfig-based auth, OIDC for shared installs, multi-cluster support, YAML-based resource creation, debugging features, and cleanup of Dashboard-specific access and RBAC.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover now supports migration from Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover Public Preview now supports cloud-to-cloud migration from Google Cloud Storage (GCS) to Azure Blob Storage. Migration uses the S3-compatible interface.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Migrate from Azure Blueprints by January 31, 2027
item.description:
Azure Blueprints retirement has been extended from July 11, 2026 to January 31, 2027. Phased retirement starts July 31, 2026, when creating new blueprint definitions will no longer be supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files migration assistant
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files migration assistant is in preview and available generally, using SnapMirror for data migration to ANF. It supports migrations from on-premises systems and from CVO or other cloud providers.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Files assessments are now available worldwide using Azure Migrate
item.description:
Azure Migrate now generally supports discovery and assessment of SMB and NFS file shares on Windows and Linux servers worldwide. The update provides a data-driven view of file share environments and helps customers plan migration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DocumentDB Migration Extension in Visual Studio Code
item.description:
Azure DocumentDB Migration Extension for Visual Studio Code is now generally available. It lets you create, run, and manage migration jobs from VS Code to move data from on-premises or cloud-based MongoDB sources.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: From Kubernetes Dashboard to Headlamp: Understanding the Transition
item.description:
Kubernetes Dashboard has been archived, and this post explains how Headlamp maps to Dashboard workflows while adding multi-cluster support, application-centric views, plugins, and flexible deployment options. It also notes that a step-by-step migration guide will follow.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover Blob-to-Blob migration
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now generally available for Blob container-to-Blob container migrations. It supports moving data across regions, subscriptions, and accounts through a fully managed service.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Schedule one-time or recurring migrations with Azure Storage Mover
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover now includes built-in job scheduling for migrations. Users can configure one-time or recurring jobs to start automatically at a specified date and time.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot Studio upgraded its WebAssembly engine to .NET 10. The migration was described as straightforward, simplified deployment, and improved performance for end users.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029
item.description:
Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029. Users should migrate to the latest generally available v4.0 API before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates
